zh.homeIntro1: 帕伊西奥斯长老（Elder Paisios，1924年7月25日—1994年7月12日），俗名阿尔塞尼奥斯·埃兹涅皮迪斯，是希腊东正教最受尊敬的当代圣徒之一。他出生于土耳其卡帕多西亚的法拉萨（今土耳其境内），幼年随家人迁居希腊。
zh.homeIntro2: 帕伊西奥斯长老一生大部分时间在阿索斯圣山隐修，以其深邃的灵修造诣和对来访者的谆谆教导闻名。他的教诲由弟子和访客记录整理，涉及灵性生活、末世征兆、地缘政治等广泛议题。2015年1月13日，他被君士坦丁堡普世牧首区正式封圣。
zh.homeIntro3: 帕伊西奥斯长老关于土耳其命运、君士坦丁堡回归、俄土冲突、欧盟解体和电子身份证（兽印）等话题的预言在东正教世界广为流传。本站整理了他最具代表性的预言，并对已到期的预言进行了真实度评估。
zh.coreQuote: 「事态将发展到一定地步后，俄罗斯人将占领土耳其。中国的三分之一将覆灭，欧洲的三分之一将受到震撼。君士坦丁堡将归还给我们。」
keyPointsList: 1924年 —— 出生于土耳其法拉萨（卡帕多西亚），受洗名阿尔塞尼奥斯
1950年代 —— 在阿索斯圣山开始隐修生活
1970-1994年 —— 预言记录期，弟子和访客记录其教导与预言
1994年 —— 于希腊萨洛尼卡附近安息
2015年 —— 被君士坦丁堡普世牧首封圣
sourceList: 预言内容主要来自弟子著述《帕伊西奥斯长老言行录》（Athonite Fathers & Elder Paisios Series）及访客回忆（Wikipedia）
英文资料参考 Mystagogy Resource Center 等东正教研究网站
验证评价基于公开新闻报道和历史记录
编者观点不代表学术定论或东正教官方立场
站点图标采用圣像光环（nimbus）符号——东正教圣像画中圣徒头部的金色光环是最具辨识度的圣徒标志

en.homeIntro1: Elder Paisios (July 25, 1924 – July 12, 1994), born Arsenios Eznepidis, was one of the most revered modern saints of the Greek Orthodox Church. He was born in Pharasa, Cappadocia (in present-day Turkey) and moved to Greece with his family as a child.
en.homeIntro2: Elder Paisios spent most of his life as a monk on Mount Athos, known for his profound spiritual wisdom and counsel to visitors. His teachings were recorded by disciples and visitors, covering spiritual life, signs of the end times, and geopolitical events. He was officially canonized by the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople on January 13, 2015.
en.homeIntro3: Elder Paisios's prophecies about the fate of Turkey, the return of Constantinople, the Russia-Turkey conflict, the dissolution of the EU, and electronic ID cards (the mark of the beast) are widely circulated in the Orthodox world. This site compiles his most representative prophecies and evaluates those whose deadlines have passed.
en.coreQuote: "Events will reach a point where the Russians will take Turkey. A third of China will perish, a third of Europe will be shaken. Constantinople will be given back to us."
keyPointsList: 1924 —— Born in Pharasa, Cappadocia (present-day Turkey), baptized Arsenios
1950s —— Began monastic life on Mount Athos
1970-1994 —— Prophecy recording period, disciples and visitors documented his teachings
2015 —— Canonized by the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople
sourceList: Prophecies sourced from disciple writings (Athonite Fathers & Elder Paisios Series) and visitor recollections (Wikipedia)
English references from Mystagogy Resource Center and other Orthodox research sites
Verification based on public news reports and historical records
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus or official Orthodox positions
Site icon: halo (nimbus) symbol — the golden halo around a saint's head in Orthodox iconography is the most recognizable symbol of sainthood
